# skill-issue

> A linter for coding-agent skill ACTIVATION. A skill is picked by its always-on
> name + description; it can be perfectly built and still never fire because the
> description is too vague to match how people phrase requests, or because it
> collides with a more specific sibling that always wins. skill-issue grades each
> skill A–F on whether it will fire, simulates which skill fires for a given
> prompt, and reports collision clusters where one skill silently shadows another.
> Heuristic and offline by default; conservative (only empty/duplicate
> descriptions are errors). Sibling to skillrot (which catches CLI drift).

Works across coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, Factory) — any
tool that loads `SKILL.md`-format skills.

## Install

- [Claude Code plugin](https://github.com/mishanefedov/skill-issue#install): `/plugin marketplace add mishanefedov/skill-issue` then `/plugin install skill-issue@skill-issue`
- [npm](https://github.com/mishanefedov/skill-issue#install): `npm install -g skill-issue` (or `npx skill-issue ~/.claude/skills`)

## Usage

- `skill-issue ~/.claude/skills` — grade every skill A–F (exit 1 on error) + collisions summary
- `skill-issue <dir> --why "<prompt>"` — simulate which skill fires for a prompt
- `skill-issue <dir> --collisions` — clusters of skills that shadow each other
- `skill-issue <dir> --fix` — append a "Use when …" clause to weak descriptions
- `skill-issue <dir> --json` — machine-readable

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/mishanefedov/skill-issue/blob/main/README.md): what it catches, how scoring works, roadmap
- [SPEC](https://github.com/mishanefedov/skill-issue/blob/main/SPEC.md): design + architecture
- [SKILL.md](https://github.com/mishanefedov/skill-issue/blob/main/skills/skill-issue/SKILL.md): the skill definition

## Scope

- Catches: vague/thin/generic descriptions, missing "use when" clauses, duplicate metadata, trigger collisions.
- Roadmap (v2): semantic (embedding) collisions, real agent-loop replay to observe selection, trigger-coverage generation.
